topic: LinearB 2026 Engineering Benchmarks: AI PR Review Takes 5.3x Longer

key_points: AI PRs wait 4.6x longer for review pickup but are reviewed 2x faster once picked up
Agentic AI PRs sit idle for 1,055 minutes vs. 201 minutes for unassisted PRs
AI PR acceptance rate 32.7% vs 84.4% for manual PRs
Focus on the wait time paradox
LinearB code review metrics framework: 8 connected dimensions
Senior developers spend 38 minutes reviewing AI PRs and accept only 23.7%
Junior developers spend 15 minutes and accept 31.9%
Elite team benchmarks: <105 lines per PR, sub-48-hour cycle times
Review capacity limits velocity regardless of AI code generation speed
Some teams see 30 PRs per day with only 6 reviewers
Code review has become the primary bottleneck for AI-native teams
Review quality and governance now determine engineering velocity
AI tools increased developer output ~60% from 2025 to 2026
A developer with AI can produce 5-6 PRs/day but reviewers handle the same volume
Teams writing more code but shipping at same or slower pace
AI tools: GitHub Copilot Code Review, CodeRabbit as solutions
8.1M PRs from 4,813 teams and 163,820 contributors across 42 countries
20 metrics spanning entire SDLC plus 3 new AI-specific metrics
Median teams: 4.2 hours focus time, 3.8-day lead times, 12.4 merged PRs/month
PR size is the most significant driver of velocity across the lifecycle

stats.fact: LinearB analyzed 8.1+ million pull requests from 4,813 teams and 163,820 contributors across 42 countries for the 2026 report
AI-Assisted PRs have review times 5.3x longer than unassisted PRs
Agentic AI PRs wait 1,055 minutes before first review vs. 201 minutes for unassisted PRs — a 5.3x gap in pickup time
AI PRs wait 4.6x longer for review pickup but are reviewed 2x faster once picked up
AI-generated PR acceptance rate: 32.7% vs. 84.4% for manual PRs
Junior developers spend 15 minutes reviewing AI PRs and accept 31.9%
Senior developers spend 38 minutes reviewing AI PRs and accept only 23.7% — less than juniors
AI-Assisted PRs are 2.6x larger than unassisted PRs on average
96% of developers do not fully trust the functional accuracy of AI-generated code
Elite teams achieve <105 lines per PR, sub-48-hour cycle times, and >6 hours of protected deep work daily
Median teams average 4.2 hours focus time, 3.8-day lead times, and 12.4 merged PRs per month
Median cycle time is 83 hours vs. elite team target of sub-48 hours
AI developer output jumped ~60% from 2025 to 2026, but shipping velocity has not kept pace
The 2026 report covers 20 SDLC metrics plus 3 all-new AI-specific metrics

angles: The AI Productivity Paradox: engineers write 60% more code but teams ship at the same or slower pace — the bottleneck moved from creation to review
The 5.3x PR pickup time gap is the hidden tax on AI adoption that engineering leaders are not tracking
Senior vs. junior developer trust gap: senior devs spend 2.5x more time on AI PRs but accept them at lower rates, revealing quality skepticism
Why DORA metrics alone are misleading in the AI era — deployment frequency can rise while real quality degrades
The elite team playbook: small PRs (<105 lines), protected focus time (6+ hours), sub-48h cycle time — and how AI fits in
Agentic AI vs. AI-Assisted PRs: the difference in review dynamics and what it means for governance
How teams should restructure review processes to keep pace with AI-generated code volume
